taxonomy: Soroseris hirsuta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Cichorieae and the genus Soroseris. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, specifically in China, Kyrgyzstan, and Tajikistan. The classification of Soroseris hirsuta is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis. Further research is needed to confirm its exact taxonomic position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Soroseris hirsuta, also known as the hairy soroseris, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling.
